workiqQueriesRun: What email threads did I send or receive most frequently in the past 7 days? List subject patterns, sender/recipient groups, and approximate time spent.
Are there recurring email types I write regularly like status updates, approvals, scheduling requests, or data requests? Show examples from the past week.
Which emails required the most back-and-forth (most replies in a thread) this past week, and what was the topic?
What recurring meetings did I attend this past week? For each, what was the typical agenda type and who attends?
How much total time did I spend in meetings this past week, broken down by meeting type (1:1, team sync, external, etc.)?
Which meeting types happen every week at roughly the same time with the same people?
What Teams channels or chats am I most active in over the past 7 days? What topics come up repeatedly?
Are there questions I get asked repeatedly in Teams chats - things people regularly come to me for?
What types of information do I most frequently share or look up in Teams conversations this week?
What types of documents did I create, edit, or review most often this past week? Any recurring doc types like reports, specs, or decks?
Are there documents I update on a regular cadence (weekly/monthly reports, trackers, dashboards)?
What SharePoint sites or OneDrive folders do I access most frequently?
Across email, meetings, and Teams, what topics or projects consumed the most of my time this week?
Are there workflows that seem to repeat - where I do the same sequence of actions?
What tasks do multiple people on my team seem to do independently that could be standardized?

signals.title: Azure DevOps Work Item Notification Triage
signals.rawExcerpt: By far the largest cluster in inbox (~200 emails in 7 days). Subject patterns: Task, Bug, Requirement, Feature, Epic with ADO numbers. Senders: azuredevops@microsoft.com, Teamspace Build Service, service accounts. System-generated, not conversational. Attention per item: low.

signals.title: Evals and Copilot Cross-Team Strategic Email Threads
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject patterns: Customer donated evals added Playbook, CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive, Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites, EvalCon Register propose content. DLs of 5-30+ people. Strategic cross-team alignment threads.

signals.title: External Partner and Customer Email Coordination
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject patterns include EXTERNAL Expeditors plus MSFT screen share, app health and credential follow-ups. Low frequency but important.

signals.title: Compliance Security and System Alert Processing
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject patterns: Action Required File Blocked, Loop workspace compliance alerts, repo permission change notifications. Often trigger action outside email in OneDrive, Loop, or GitHub.

signals.title: Loop Workspace Collaboration Invitation Emails
signals.rawExcerpt: Most frequent outbound email type: You have been looped in - auto-generated workspace invitations. 2 out of 3 sent emails this week matched this pattern.

signals.title: Urgent Administrative Escalation Email Drafting
signals.rawExcerpt: Manually written high-urgency email to resolve a blocking issue sent to external administrative inbox. Tone: urgent, direct. Manual emails are written for high-stakes exception handling.

signals.title: Project Crucible Deep-Dive Multi-Reply Email Thread
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject: CAPE East Project Crucible Deepdive Evals-first Mindset Progress and Learnings 18th March. Very large DL including CAPE leadership EVALS XLT and EH stakeholders. References engaged discussion follow-ups action items and shared decks.

signals.title: URGENT Declarative Agent Architecture Escalation Thread
signals.rawExcerpt: Subject: URGENT DBS Declarative Agent grounded in 170+ SharePoint sites. Explicitly marked URGENT with long CC list. Topic: declarative agent grounding against very large SharePoint corpus scalability correctness and risk.

signals.title: Inbound Email to Meeting to Artifact Creation Workflow
signals.rawExcerpt: Most consistent repeating workflow: email thread with guidance or alignment request leads to proposing a meeting then calendar invite then follow-up emails summarizing decisions plus documents or templates created. Meta-pattern: Sense-make then Align then Codify.

signals.title: Weekly US Pod Stand-Up
signals.rawExcerpt: US Pod Stand-Up: Tuesdays 12:35-1:00 PM. Classic weekly stand-up structure with stable leadership group. Same organizer same required attendees fixed weekly slot.

signals.title: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team Sync
signals.rawExcerpt: Weekly Project Crucible v-Team: Mondays 8:05-8:30 AM. Explicitly weekly same attendees same time window. Cross-functional but consistent v-team organized by manager.

signals.title: Weekly CAPE Architecture Review Call
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Architecture Call: Mondays 9:05-10:00 AM. Weekly architectural review with stable distribution list. CAPE FTE and consistent leadership set.

signals.title: Weekly CAPE Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Sync
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Copilot Studio Customer Engagement Sync: Fridays 10:00-11:00 AM. Long-running weekly recurrence with fixed scope and group. Same customer-engagement leads week over week.

signals.title: Weekly MCS Analytics Evaluations PM Review
signals.rawExcerpt: MCS Analytics Evaluations CAPECAT-PM Review: Wednesdays 9:00-9:30 AM. Weekly review cadence with the same PM cohort. Consistent PM and analytics leadership set.

signals.title: Copilot Studio Technical Office Hours Weekly 2x
signals.rawExcerpt: Copilot Studio Technical Office Hours: Tuesdays 8:05-9:00 AM and Wednesdays 1:05-2:00 AM. Weekly time-locked same owner and technical audience. Although labeled office hours recur weekly at same time with same hosts.

signals.title: SEVAL BizChat Office Hours Weekly US and EU Asia
signals.rawExcerpt: SEVAL BizChat Office Hours: Tuesdays 5:00-5:30 PM US and Wednesdays 1:00-1:30 AM EU/Asia. Predictable weekly cadence with identical host lists.

signals.title: Weekly Learning and Scaling Fridays Session
signals.rawExcerpt: Learning and Scaling Fridays: Fridays 2:05-5:00 PM. Same organizer same required attendees fixed weekly slot. One of the strongest heartbeat meetings with stable attendance.

signals.title: Weekly BAT Team Casual Get-Together
signals.rawExcerpt: BAT Team Casual Get-Together: Thursdays 4:00-4:30 PM. Weekly recurrence with stable optional list same slot. Team culture informal but consistent cadence.

signals.title: Repeated Customer Engagement Intake and Qualification Discussions
signals.rawExcerpt: Multiple meetings debate the same intake questions: readiness access data availability nomination criteria who qualifies first and when to say no. Questions re-asked verbally rather than captured up front. Repeated friction and late discovery of blockers.

signals.title: Repeated Eval Strategy and Depth Consultations in Teams
signals.rawExcerpt: People repeatedly ask: How many eval cases should we run per scenario? Are we over-doing evals or under-doing them? How deep should we go for high-risk vs low-risk scenarios? Appears across field conversations PM syncs leadership prep and customer escalations. Explicitly referenced as the person others check with for eval guidance.

signals.title: Recurring Requests for Eval Decks Docs and Templates
signals.rawExcerpt: People regularly ask for the latest eval deck and whether older ones are outdated one-pager eval instructions for specific scenarios playbooks checklists templates and where something lives on SharePoint or GitHub. Shows up repeatedly with questions about which version is current.

signals.title: Eval Approach Sanity-Check Reviews
signals.rawExcerpt: Consistent pattern: people bring draft thinking and ask if it makes sense. Examples: Are these the right graders to use? Does this checklist look right? Does this framework hold up? Happens across PMs engineers and field partners before they circulate something more broadly. Trusted for judgment and taste.

signals.title: Org Routing and Escalation Path Clarifications
signals.rawExcerpt: People come when unsure who owns a given eval feature or API how to route feature requests vs quality issues which team or DRI to involve. Examples: CRM routing chat APIs SuccessHub access ownership clarification. Functioning as an informal systems map for the org.

signals.title: Repeated Sharing of Eval Frameworks Playbooks and Technical Artifacts
signals.rawExcerpt: Repeatedly shared evaluation playbooks scenario libraries and templates including eval scenario repos triage and improvement playbook eval set CSV templates. Discussed evaluation gaps especially actionability of eval results criteria drift and acceptance criteria definition.

signals.title: Agent Skills and Skill-Discovery Collaboration Threads
signals.rawExcerpt: Very active in hands-on agent and skills discussions: sharing GitHub repos ZIPs dashboards YAML/MD files. Discussing skills-detective skill-creator PRD generators live iteration and rapid prototyping. Repeatedly framed skills as reusable building blocks rather than full agents or apps.

signals.title: Product Roadmap and GA Status Clarification Requests
signals.rawExcerpt: Repeated pattern of people pinging to clarify GA vs public preview status of Copilot Studio eval features what is coming next (multi-grader custom graders rubric changes) whether something is safe to recommend to customers. Appears in PM chats field enablement threads and last-minute leadership asks.

signals.title: Team-Wide Manual CRM Status Update Hygiene
signals.rawExcerpt: Standups and pod meetings repeatedly surface manual status updates stage corrections and questions like should we change the engagement type in CRM. Different people updating independently often discovering inconsistencies only during meetings. Same task same system same fields done by many people independently.

signals.title: Ad-Hoc Cross-Team One-Line Status Summary Requests
signals.rawExcerpt: Ad-hoc asks for quick summaries such as can you give me one line on eval status pop up repeatedly. The same context is rewritten for different stakeholders. High frequency low leverage writing. Context switching cost is high. Could be standardized as a rolling weekly blurb per initiative.

signals.title: HTML Dashboard Cycle-Based Refresh Skilluminator and CAPE Eval
signals.rawExcerpt: Skilluminator and Skill-Detector Dashboards: explicitly cycle-based HTML dashboards (Cycle 3 weekly analysis). CAPE Eval Dashboard shows multiple files repeated sharing and recent modifications. These are true dashboards regenerated and republished on a cadence.

signals.title: Agent Eval Progress Report Periodic Versioning
signals.rawExcerpt: Agent_Eval_Progress_Report v3 and v4: Word document tracking progress from eval framework to customer execution. Multiple versions edited within the same reporting window in March 2026. Functions as a recurring status report rather than a one-off doc.

signals.title: CAPE Eval Customer Engagement Tracker Weekly Updates
signals.rawExcerpt: CAPE Eval Engagement Tracker: Loop tracker for active customer eval engagements agent types tooling and notes. Cadence: ongoing often weekly as engagements change. Co-author; tracker actively maintained with dated rows and customer updates.

signals.title: Review ROB Strategy Slide Deck Creation and Editing
signals.rawExcerpt: Repeatedly created edited or reviewed presentation decks: working decks for reviews planning and readouts (MPRs engineering reviews PM RoBs) strategy and pitch decks tied to Copilot and agents platform direction event and hackathon prep decks. Single most recurring doc type.

signals.title: Redundant ROB Leadership Review Deck Recreation Across Team
signals.rawExcerpt: Repeated creation and editing of ROB decks and status slides with confusion about which deck is canonical. People ask Which deck are we adding to or re-add similar content (wins blockers metrics) across different reviews. Same structure every time. Time-consuming and highly duplicative.
